News App
  • News App Documentation
  • View page source

News App Documentation

Database models for the News App.

This module defines the core data structures used by the application: - Publisher: organizations that publish articles - User: custom user model with a role (Reader, Journalist, Editor) and subscription relations - Article: news article workflow with PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED states

class core.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

A news article with an editorial workflow.

Status flow:

PENDING -> APPROVED or REJECTED

Attributes:

title (str): Article title. body (str): Article body text. publisher (Publisher): The publisher associated with the article. author (User): The journalist who wrote the article. status (str): One of Status choices. decision_reason (str): Optional reason provided when rejecting. decided_at (datetime): Timestamp when approved/rejected. created_at (datetime): Created timestamp. updated_at (datetime): Updated timestamp.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

class Status(*values)[source]

Bases: TextChoices

Enumerated status values for article moderation.

APPROVED = 'APPROVED'
PENDING = 'PENDING'
REJECTED = 'REJECTED'
approve() → None[source]

Mark the article as approved and set the decision timestamp.

Returns:

None

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id
reject(reason: str = '') → None[source]

Mark the article as rejected and store a rejection reason.

Args:

reason (str): Optional editorial reason for the rejection.

Returns:

None

class core.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

A publisher that can have articles associated with it.

Attributes:

name (str): Human-readable publisher name. description (str): Optional description/bio for the publisher.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

description

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

name

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
subscribers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

class core.models.User(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model with roles and subscription relationships.

Roles:

  • READER: can subscribe to publishers/journalists and access reader API feeds

  • JOURNALIST: can submit articles (PENDING)

  • EDITOR: can approve/reject articles

Attributes:

role (str): One of the Role choices. bio (str): Optional user bio. subscribed_publishers (ManyToMany[Publisher]): Publishers this user subscribes to. subscribed_journalists (ManyToMany[User]): Journalists this user follows.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

class Role(*values)[source]

Bases: TextChoices

Enumerated roles used for permission checks and UI display.

EDITOR = 'EDITOR'
JOURNALIST = 'JOURNALIST'
READER = 'READER'
articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

username

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

Views for the News App.

This module contains Django view functions for: - reader browsing (home, article detail) - subscriptions (publishers, journalists, my subscriptions) - journalist workflows (dashboard, create article) - editor workflows (review queue, approve/reject)

core.views.article_detail(request, pk: int)[source]

Display a single approved article by primary key.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest. pk (int): Article primary key.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered article detail page.

core.views.article_list(request)[source]

Display the public homepage article list (APPROVED only).

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered article list.

core.views.create_article(request)[source]

Journalist-only view to submit a new article (created as PENDING).

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered create form on GET; redirect on success.

Raises:

HttpResponseForbidden: If user is not a journalist.

core.views.create_publisher(request)[source]

Editor-only view to create publishers that journalists can publish under.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered create publisher form or redirect on success.

Raises:

HttpResponseForbidden: If user is not an editor.

core.views.decide_article(request, pk: int)[source]

Editor-only action endpoint to approve or reject an article.

  • Approve: sets APPROVED, emails author, emails publisher subscribers, attempts X post

  • Reject: sets REJECTED, saves reason, emails author

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest. pk (int): Article primary key.

Returns:

HttpResponseRedirect: Redirect back to editor queue.

Raises:

HttpResponseForbidden: If user is not an editor.

core.views.editor_queue(request)[source]

Editor-only review queue showing all pending articles.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered editor queue page.

Raises:

HttpResponseForbidden: If user is not an editor.

core.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]

Journalist-only dashboard showing their submitted articles.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered journalist dashboard.

Raises:

HttpResponseForbidden: If user is not a journalist.

core.views.journalist_list(request)[source]

List all journalists and show which ones the current user follows.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered journalist list page.

core.views.my_subscriptions(request)[source]

Display a summary of the current user’s publisher subscriptions and journalist follows.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered subscriptions page.

core.views.publisher_list(request)[source]

List all publishers and show which ones the current user is subscribed to.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered publisher list page.

core.views.register(request)[source]

Register a new user account.

Behavior:

  • Creates a user account and assigns a role based on the registration form.

  • Role selection is limited to Reader/Journalist to prevent privilege escalation.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest.

Returns:

HttpResponse: Rendered registration form or redirect on success.

core.views.toggle_journalist_follow(request, pk: int)[source]

Follow/unfollow a journalist user.

Expects POST. Non-POST requests are redirected back to journalist list.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest. pk (int): Journalist user primary key.

Returns:

HttpResponseRedirect: Redirect back to journalist list.

core.views.toggle_publisher_subscription(request, pk: int)[source]

Subscribe/unsubscribe the current user to a publisher.

Expects POST. Non-POST requests are redirected back to publisher list.

Args:

request: Django HttpRequest. pk (int): Publisher primary key.

Returns:

HttpResponseRedirect: Redirect back to publisher list.

class core.forms.ArticleForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for journalists to create a new article.

Bases: object

model

alias of Article

declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class core.forms.PublisherForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for editors to create a Publisher through the web UI.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['name', 'description']
model

alias of Publisher

base_fields = {'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'name': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class core.forms.RegistrationForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: UserCreationForm

Registration form for creating a new user account.

Users can register as Reader or Journalist freely. Editor registration requires a valid invite code.

class Meta[source]

Bases: Meta

fields = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'editor_invite_code')
model

alias of User

base_fields = {'editor_invite_code': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}
clean()[source]

Validate Editor signups require a correct invite code.

declared_fields = {'editor_invite_code': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit: bool = True)[source]

Create the user and assign the selected role.

Args:

commit: Whether to save the user to the database.

Returns:

The created user instance.
